# Cloud Waste Scanner Community v2.9.23

v2.9.23 is a community client and content operations update.  
This release opens more of the Team workflow surface in the client repository, adds operator-facing governance handoff paths, and fixes website/release content alignment without changing desktop binary metadata.

## What changed

### Team workflow surface in Community source

- Added finding owner roles:
  - `owner`
  - `manager`
- Added Team workspace governance support behind edition gates:
  - org structure
  - owner directory
  - finding lifecycle workflows
  - org summary in governance exports
- Added handoff pack generation from Scan Results:
  - `summary.txt`
  - `resources.csv`
  - `manifest.json`
  - audience-targeted templates for `exec`, `owner`, and `audit`
- Added manager-only batch assignment flow in Scan Results.
- Added org-unit and owner filters in Scan Results.
- Added runtime operator role hardening:
  - role setting moved from frontend-only trust to backend enforcement
  - local admin whitelist guard for runtime role changes
  - audit path coverage for runtime role configuration
- Added Team operator documentation:
  - `TEAM_USER_GUIDE.md`
  - `TEAM_PERMISSIONS_AND_OPERATIONS.md`
  - `RESULT_HANDOFF_MVP.md`

### Blog and site content operations

- Updated newest blog spotlight target to:
  - `A 30-Day Cloud Waste Turnaround: What Actually Worked`
- Corrected blog index metadata alignment:
  - `Last updated` and `Newest post` now reference the same newest article.
- Corrected blog series placement:
  - Moved the 30-day turnaround article into Incident/Story context.
  - Removed this article from Release Notes series context.
- Corrected Release Notes series ordering to chronological sequence for forward growth.
- Reconciled blog series card counts and per-series totals for consistency.
- Added prominent support entry points:
  - GitHub README sponsor/coffee badges
  - website homepage sponsor CTA
  - website blog/blog-series sponsor links

### Release process hardening

- Added `v2.9.23` release note as a first-class tracked release artifact.
- Enforced release notes gating in CI:
  - tagged release now requires `.github/release-notes/<tag>.md`
  - missing release note file blocks release workflow.
- Configured GitHub release body generation from tag-matched release note file.
- Clarified that this release is non-binary and keeps desktop app metadata unchanged.

## Compatibility

- App metadata version: unchanged from previous binary build.
- Scope: community client workflow surface + website/blog content operations + release governance.

## Delivery notes

- This release identifier is intended for private release tracking continuity when no new desktop binaries are produced.
- Future release tags continue from this sequence while preserving binary/content separation rules.
